blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Last session. I don't want to give you a summary or a list. I want to end with one question and I want you both to actually answer it.
She looked at them through the screen.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'm promising to tell the truth even when it's hard. I'm promising to not go quiet when he needs to hear me. I'm promising to stay in the room — to not let the distance between us stay comfortable enough that we stop trying to close it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'm promising to love him on the days when it's easy. And on the days when it's a choice.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'm promising to show up. Not the version of me that seems okay. The actual one. I'm promising to let her into the hard parts — to not disappear when something costs me. To not protect her from me.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'm promising that I will not be my father. Not by accident. On purpose. Every day — on purpose.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: That is what a covenant is. Not a feeling. Not a ceremony. A decision, remade. Every morning. On purpose.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: You are ready. You have done the work. Whatever comes next — you have the vocabulary for it now. Go get married.
